extraction.one_line_summary: KWIATKOWSKI Michal won from the breakaway after POGACAR attacked in final kilometer, taking time bonus to close on VINGEGAARD's yellow jersey.
tactical_patterns: UAE controlled gap at 1:26-1:27 entire stage to set up Pogacar's late bonus sprint, never allowing break freedom.
Pogacar left attack until final 500m of Grand Colombier, targeting only time bonus not stage win—energy conservation tactic.
Vingegaard cracked on Pogacar's final acceleration at finish line, first weakness signal after three mountain stages under pressure.
POWLESS deliberately sacrificed KOM points on Grand Colombier finish, banking energy for Stage 14's 52-point mountain haul.

rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: positive: survived despite UAE's sustained pressure on 17.5km climb, beat breakaway companions with 4-second margin in uphill sprint
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: third consecutive mountain stage taking time from Vingegaard, now using late micro-attacks for bonus seconds rather than sustained efforts
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: cracked in final 500m kick for first time, lost bonus seconds—Visma relying on higher Alpine stages to reverse trend
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: tactical maturity to skip finish points, recognizing Stage 14's category one climbs offer superior KOM opportunity
rider_signals.SHAW James: positive: breakaway rider salvaged second place and bonus seconds after Kwiatkowski's attack, smart opportunism from domestique role
